$comment: Cross-cutting questions asked during bootstrap. Subagent-specific questions are owned by each subagent, not centralized here. Each question has a stable id, an intent, and an expected answer shape. The elicitation rendering is environment-specific.
purpose: Identity and mode questions shared across multiple subagents, asked once by the orchestrator.

questions.intent: Confirm the auto-detected project mode (greenfield, greenfield-from-empty, or brownfield).
questions.notes: Presented with the signals the agent observed. User may override when detection is wrong.

questions.intent: Project name (defaults to inferred from manifest or directory name).
questions.notes: Pre-fill from package.json/Cargo.toml/etc. User confirms or overrides.

questions.intent: Subject-verb-user-outcome sentence describing what this product lets its user do, under what constraint.
questions.notes: Hard requirement. Agent rejects generic statements and pushes back for specificity.

questions.intent: Concrete archetype(s) of primary users. Not 'developers'; a specific role with context.
questions.notes: Agent probes vague answers. Primary users must not be 'everyone'.

questions.intent: Observable outcomes that mean this product is working.
questions.notes: Outcomes must be observable. Feelings or intentions do not count.

questions.intent: Things this project will explicitly not do, each paired with a reason.

expected_response.description: Array of {goal, reason} objects (preferred). Bare strings accepted for backwards compatibility.

example.reason: library extraction only — OCR would widen scope beyond the product's reason for existing.
batch cadence is a deliberate simplicity commitment; real-time invites state-machine complexity we refuse to own.

questions.notes: Agent probes for reasons. An anti-goal without a reason remains a preference; it must become a commitment to enter the contract.

questions.intent: Confirm detected build / test / lint / typecheck commands, or provide them.
questions.notes: Non-blocking. When not provided, contract keeps nulls; post-bootstrap maintenance may fill later.

questions.intent: When no ambiguity policy matches, must the agent halt and escalate, or proceed with labeled judgment?
questions.notes: Default is halt_and_escalate. Any override to proceed_with_judgment records explicitly.

questions.intent: Confirm which discovered agentic files may be patched with the constitution-first block.
questions.notes: The adapter subagent populates options at runtime from its heuristic scan. User may approve all, approve some, or defer with 'none'.
